summary: Folic acid supplementation should begin at least three months prior to attempting pregnancy due to its importance in preventing neural tube defects within the first trimester when many pregnancies are unplanned or unrecognized until later stages; this timing allows sufficient time for adequate vitamin stores which can prevent such issues effectively according to current medical recommendations aimed primarily toward reducing birth defect risk through proactive health measures. The recommendation emphasizes starting pre-conceptionally rather than waiting till after confirming pregnancy as it ensures better compliance given potential delays between planning parenthood and actual fertilization events occurring naturally without artificial interventions like IVF procedures where specific dosages may differ based upon individual circumstances requiring personalized advice tailored accordingly while maintaining overall public health goals promoting widespread use among reproductive age groups universally regardless their method chosen leading up to successful live births free from major congenital anomalies whenever possible under normal conditions barring exceptional cases needing specialized attention outside standard practice parameters set forth herein
